titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Department of History, Yale University, Senior Prize Essays
Department of History, Yale University, Senior Prize Essays
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 20, 2018 at 07:27
finding aid revisiion description not supplied

did.unittitle: Department of History, Yale University, senior prize essays
did.abstract: The materials consist of senior prize essays transferred by the Department of History since 1991. A few essays by non-seniors are also included.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 14-B (YRG 14-B), Records of academic departments and programs of the Yale Faculty of Arts and Sciences.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The materials were transferred from the Department of History, 1991-2018.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been transferred to Yale University. Requires written permission of the author or copyright holder to photocopy.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Department of History, Yale University, Senior Prize Essays (RU 170). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged and described at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does no further arrangement and description at the accession level.

originalsloc.head: Existence and Location of Originals
originalsloc.p: Senior prize essays transferred prior to 1991 are classed in the Yale Student Papers Collection (RU 331).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original born digital files, as well as preservation masters, may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist request that they be made. Born digital files cannot be accessed remotely. System requirements include a Manuscripts and Archives computer and file viewing software.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of senior prize essays transferred by the Department of History since 1991. A few essays by non-seniors are also included.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged by accession.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The essays are arranged chronologically by academic year and alphabetically by author therein. Non-senior essay writers are indicated by a class year in parentheses after their name. The name of the prize, if known, is listed in italics.

did.unittitle: Breaks, Katherine. "The Ladies of the OSS: The Apron Strings of Intelligence in World War II"
Jacobs, Greg. "What We Must Do Well: Implementation vs. Desegregation in the Columbus Public School System, 1973-1985"
Pollak, Harrison. "European Naturalists and the Emerging Idea of the Amazon, 1743-1866"
Student essays awarded the Richard Hegel Prize on topics related to New Haven

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accession consists of student essays awarded or honorably mentioned for the Richard Hegel Prize for essays related to New Haven.

The Manuscripts and Archives Prize (for outstanding research done in Manuscripts and Archives)

History of Science and Medicine prize winners
History of Science and Medicine prize winners
Ewing-Nelson, Sinclair, "Gun Violence and Gun Control in Coleman Young's Detroit".
Judt, Daniel, "The Reactionary Dilemma: George Fitzhugh and the Fight for a Southern Future, 1850-1870".
Black, Griffin, "Lion at the Lectern: Frederick Douglass Becomes an Orator, 1938-1945".
Ranieri, Kyle, "Restoring Hozho: Indigenous Constitutionalism in the Navajo Tribal Courts".
The George Washington Egleston American Prize
Samora, Chelsea, "Advancement in Every Direction, Except Suffrage: Female Anti-Suffragists and Conceptions of Political Womanhood in the United States, 1880-1920".
The John Addison Porter American History Prize
Raik, Rohan, "Para un Futuro Mejor: The Evolution of Puerto Rican Identity, Activism and Politics in New York City from 1950-1964".
Xiao, Emily, "Between Incarceration and Care: Disability, Bureaucracy, and Citizenship in Japanese American Wartime Camps, 1942-1946".
Augustine, John A., "Framing the Public: James J. Hill and the great Northern Railroad's Public-Benefit Strategy Between 1885-1917".
Ramsden, Walter McLean, "Action Intellectuals and Responsible Bureaucracy in Twentieth-Century America: The Ideas and Institution-Building Projects of Carl Joachim Friedrich".
Gurwitt, Sam, "The Egyptian Facade of the German Democratic Republic: Cultural Politics of East Berlin's Egyptian Museum, 1953-1989".
Shimer, David, "The Stasi's Operation to Save Ostpolitik: Bribery, Corruption, and the Near Downfall of West German Chancellor Willy Brandt".
Adler, Jonathan, "The Iraq Petroleum Company in Mandatory Palestine, 1927-1939".
Walsh, Caitlin, "All that Glitters is Not Gold: The Rise and Fall of Early British Colonial Banking Schemes in Massachusetts, Jamaica and Barbados".
History of Science and Medicine prize winners
Martin Klein and George Rosen Prize
Ngo, Nealie, "The Body Issue: What Global and Historical Perspectives of the Ideal Female Body Can Teach Us About Our Own Present-Day Bodies".
Wingerson, Mary, "Lunacy Under the Burden of Freedom: Race and Insanity in the American South, 1840-1890".
Latin American, Africa, Asia & Middle East Prize
Benares, Isabel, "Technocractic Rule Under Ferdinand Marcos: the Men Behind His "New Society"".
Nasrallah, Lena, "Contested Space: Refugees, the Law, and the Shaping of Post-Partition Calcutta".
The Max Bildner Prize for Latin American History
Anderson, Elena, "Seven Million Colored Condoms: Population and Family Planning Initiatives in the Dominican Republic, 1965-1985".
Kuritzkes, Caroline, "Tourism, Democracy, and the Cuban Revolution after the Soviet Collapse: Economic Reform in the "Special Period", 1989-1995".
Tan, Mitchell, "Spiritual Fraternities: The Transnational Networks of Ngo-Dinh Diem's Personalist Revolution and South Vietnam's Republic, 1955-1963".
Gutfreund, Hedy, "Somewhere in the Middle-Between: Opportunities and Limitations for Refugee Scholars at Black Colleges"
Bernstein, Will, "From Paris to New Haven: Maurice Rotival and the Longue Duree of Urban Renewal".
Sweedler, Maya, "Why were you not Zusya": Maurice Freedman and the Americanization of Martin Buber"".
The Wolfgang Leonhard Prize in Russian and East Europe Studies
Sveshnikov, Stephan, "Politics and Daily Life at the First Session of the Sobor of 1917-1918".
